figure:
Install galvanized pipe culverts
(0010) 12 to 24 inches
(0020) 26 to 45 inches
(0030) 48 to 72 inches
Install galvanized culvert (bolted)
(0040) 12 to 24 inches
(0050) 26 to 45 inches
(0060) 48 to 72 inches
Minimum suggested crew size: three builders and one equipment operator.

Notes:
1. Installation of galvanized culvert includes unloading, fine grading, placing, caulking, and installing joint
clamps.
2. Installation of galvanized culvert (bolted) includes bolting together of sections, unloading, fine grading,
and placement.
3. Man-hour estimates for galvanized culvert (bolted) are based on sections being bolted into desired
lengths in a prefabrication yard.
4. When installing culverts over 48 inches in diameter, it is recommended that cross bridging be used to
prevent culverts from being bent or twisted during hauling or installation. Cross bridging can easily be
removed upon completion of backfilling and compaction.

(0010) Install culvert denial, Type I, 18 inch and smaller
(0012) Install anchor, Type I, 18 inch and smaller
(0015) Install culvert denial, Type I, 21 inch and larger
(0017) Install anchor, Type I, 21 inch and larger
(0020) Install culvert denial, Type II
(0030) Install culvert denial, Type III
(0032) Install anchor, Type II and III
(0040) Install culvert denial, Type IIIM
(0042) Install anchor, Type IIIM
Minimum suggested crew size: one equipment operator and one to two laborers.
